content.problemStatement:
Appy and Chef are participating in a contest. There are NNN problems in this contest; each problem has a unique problem code between 111 and NNN inclusive. Appy and Chef decided to split the problems to solve between them â€• Appy should solve the problems whose problem codes are divisible by AAA but not divisible by BBB, and Chef should solve the problems whose problem codes are divisible by BBB but not divisible by AAA (they decided to not solve the problems whose codes are divisible by both AAA and BBB).

To win, it is necessary to solve at least KKK problems. You have to tell Appy whether they are going to win or lose.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains four space-separated integers NNN, AAA, BBB and KKK.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the string `"Win"` if they can solve at least KKK problems or `"Lose"` otherwise (without quotes).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Appy is solving the problems with codes 222 and 444, Chef is solving the problem with code 333. Nobody is solving problem 666, since 666 is divisible by both 222 and 333. Therefore, they can solve 333 problems and win.
